title: Supabase raises $500M Series F at $10.5B as AI agents drive backend demand
subtitle: GIC leads the round seven months after a $5B Series E, with Salesforce Ventures joining as the agentic-infrastructure thesis goes mainstream.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Supabase announced a $500 million Series F at a $10.5 billion post-money valuation on June 4, 2026. GIC led the round. Existing investors Accel, Y Combinator, Craft, Felicis, Peak XV, Coatue, and Stripe all participated, and Salesforce Ventures joined as a new investor. The round arrives just seven months after a $100 million Series E that valued the company at $5 billion — a doubling in two quarters. Total funding now exceeds $1 billion. Alongside the announcement, Supabase released a preview of Multigres, an open-source horizontal scaling layer for Postgres aimed at teams that have outgrown a single database instance.

Supabase's growth curve is one of the cleanest available readouts on whether the agentic-coding wave is producing real workloads. Six years ago Supabase was a Firebase alternative; today it's the default Postgres-plus-auth-plus-storage stack that AI-generated apps land on. Vibe coding — prompt-to-deployed-product workflows — needs a backend that an agent can provision in a single tool call. Supabase happens to be that backend. The $10.5 billion valuation is a market vote that this isn't a side effect of the AI boom, it's a category of its own.

The same week's enterprise-software round table also included Ramp at $750 million, Flourish at $500 million, AlphaSense at $350 million, and Suno at $400 million. Q1 2026 venture funding hit roughly $300 billion globally, mostly chasing AI. Most of those rounds were for application-layer companies. Supabase is the rarer infrastructure-layer beneficiary — the picks-and-shovels position that doesn't depend on which specific AI app wins. As the agent boom matures, the infrastructure that agents call from will likely consolidate around two or three winners. Supabase is now firmly in that bracket.

A note for learners: if you're learning to ship with AI, the stack the agents reach for matters more than the one you'd pick by hand. Supabase, Vercel, Cloudflare, and the AI-friendly Postgres providers are increasingly the default tools that Claude, Codex, and Cursor scaffold against. Learn one of them well — it doubles as learning what your AI partner will produce when you ask it to ship the backend.
